rent.note: furnished 1BR in Yanahuara/Miraflores; range $300–$500; 20–40% cheaper than Lima

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; mild highland climate; heating occasionally needed

groceries.note: home cooking; local markets (Mercado San Camilo) exceptional value

diningOut.note: local menus $2–4; growing gastronomy scene; cheaper than Lima/Cusco

transit.note: combis + taxis; city center very walkable (flat sillar stone streets)

coworking.note: limited formal coworking; budget assumes cafe work plus occasional coworking/day access

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, Colca Canyon excursions, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Arequipa is very flat seasonally.

evidence.claim: comfortable nomad budget $1,000–$1,300/month; avg single ~$685/month bare-bones
evidence.quote: Arequipa is estimated at $1,000-1,300/month for a comfortable digital nomad lifestyle; A single person's monthly expenses are estimated at $685
similar accommodations in Arequipa might cost $300-500 for a one-bedroom flat; Cities like Arequipa have lower living costs, with rents 20%–40% cheaper than Lima

evidence.claim: Arequipa 87% cheaper than New York City
evidence.quote: Arequipa is 87% cheaper than New York City

evidence.claim: avg all-in at ~$585/month possible frugally; $1,000–1,500 comfortably
evidence.quote: average cost of living at $585; it is possible to live comfortably in Arequipa on a monthly budget of $1,000 to $1,500
